contents:
# Philippines CERT Issues Alert on Rising AI-Driven Disinformation Campaigns

## Overview
The Philippine Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-PH) warned of intensified AI-generated disinformation targeting the 2025 midterm elections. State actors allegedly used deepfakes to undermine public trust in key institutions. Authorities urged enhanced cybersecurity measures across government agencies.

## Details
The alert cited over 50 verified deepfake videos circulating on social media since April 25. Involved platforms include TikTok and Facebook, with content impersonating politicians. National Security Council coordinated with DICT for takedown operations. "We detected a 300% spike in malicious AI content," stated CERT-PH director Juan Dela Cruz. Affected regions include Metro Manila and Cebu. No casualties reported, but potential for social unrest highlighted. Training exercises for 200 cybersecurity personnel scheduled for May 2.

**Location**: Manila, Philippines

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @SindikasyonTek, which covers Philippines technology cybersecurity disinformation. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Philippines technology cybersecurity disinformation will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
